user_message: Michael Petrov at Humongous Insurance just sent this. Help me draft the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-03-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft), Priya (Humongous Insurance)

[00:02:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:06:00] Priya (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:09:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Priya (Humongous Insurance): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:21:00] Priya (Humongous Insurance): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-P trap (No native 'multi-party decision distribution' primitive); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-P failure mode (No native 'multi-party decision distribution' primitive)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-P -- No native 'multi-party decision distribution' primitive
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
